This 30% beat was driven by the strong performance of the NBA 2K franchise, with daily active users increasing by nearly 20% this quarter. Regarding Zynga and the mobile segment, mobile net bookings increased by 6%, slightly below guidance.

2025 is expected to be a major year for TTWO, with major upcoming releases such as Sid Meier’s Civilization VII on February 11, Mafia: The Old Country in the summer, Grand Theft Auto VI in autumn, and Borderlands 4 before year-end. Given this impressive pipeline, which excites gamers like me, management expects record bookings for FY 2026 and FY 2027.

For Q4 2024, management forecasts net bookings to range from $1.48 billion to $1.58 billion, supported mainly by Civilization VII and WWE 2K25.

GTA V – A True Phenomenon

GTA V has been a true phenomenon. Released in September 2013, this game stood out thanks to its graphics, realism, and longevity, characterized by multiplayer servers where players can let their imagination run wild. With more than 210 million units sold, GTA V has generated nearly $9.5 billion in revenues through game sales and in-game purchases.

An important reminder: an initial investment of $265 million was made in GTA V, representing approximately a 35x return on investment. I say "approximately" as this figure does not account for the amount spent on marketing and the release of new expansions over the years.

In terms of performance, TTWO has outperformed both the SPX and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) , especially since the release of GTA V.

GTA VI – Will it Repeat?

There are several elements that lead me to believe that GTA VI will be an even greater success than GTA V.

First, the wait has been long, the hype is real, and expectations are high. As proof of how anticipated and iconic the series is, the GTA VI trailer became the most-watched video on YouTube in 24 hours for a non-music video. The trailer now accounts for 240 million views, which, interestingly, is quite close to the total number of GTA V units sold.

Second, one of the most crucial elements is the unique positioning that the franchise allows it to exploit. GTA is at once a third-person shooter, an open-world game, and a legitimate racing game. As a sandbox-type PvP & PvE experience, it holds an almost monopolistic position after crushing competitors such as Saints Row, Just Cause, and Sleeping Dogs.

The third is respect for the community. I believe that part of GTA V’s success can be attributed to its community and the relationship that TTWO and its Rockstar Games studio have fostered with players over the years. TTWO knows what makes its games great and listens to its community, as demonstrated by recurring free content updates, the GTA RP scene, and its embrace of mods to support players while enhancing the game’s longevity.

The fourth is experience. TTWO and Rockstar Games are among the most prestigious video game studios. They know the recipe for success by heart and understand what makes their games great. Many investors fear a poor launch and a disappointing game as if nothing has been developed between GTA V and GTA VI. In reality, other great games have been developed in the meantime, notably Red Dead Redemption 2, which has sold more than 70 million units and holds a 97% score on Metacritic.

An even greater financial success than GTA V Incoming ?

I also hold the conviction that several elements will heavily support GTA VI dynamic and performance over the upcoming years.

First, the video game industry has shifted from being primarily physical in 2013 to a digital industry today. Back in 2013, almost 80% of video games were sold physically. As of today, close to 90% of video games are sold digitally. This shift from physical to digital sales implies greater profitability for game developers. It drastically reduces manufacturing costs, eliminates the need for stores and retailers who typically take a cut, and prevents reselling.

Second, the massive investment in GTA VI, nearly $2 billion, makes it the most expensive video game ever developed. That’s four times higher than Cyberpunk 2077 and four times higher than Spider-Man 2, two open-world games. This figure is even more impressive when considering how great GTA V was, with only $265 million spent on it.

Finally, an overlooked element that I believe will bring continuity and stability to GTA VI sales is the timing of its release. GTA VI is expected to be released in autumn 2025, launching first on PS5 and Xbox Series X. A year later, a PC version should follow. GTA VI will come out approximately five years after the release of the PS5 and Xbox Series X, which I see as perfect timing, as it coincides with the later stage of a console cycle, typically lasting around eight years. In other words, I wouldn’t be surprised to see players purchasing the game on the PS5 and then rebuying it on the next generation of consoles, just as many did with GTA V on the PS3 before upgrading to the PS4.

TTWO - GTA is Not The Only Strength !

GTA is on everyone's lips, but it hasn’t been TTWO’s only source of revenue for the past 10 years. From 2014 to 2024, TTWO generated close to $32 billion in revenues. This means that GTA V accounted for 30% of the firm’s revenues over the last decade. Over the years, things have changed, and TTWO has become a more diversified company, with the mobile gaming segment now accounting for approximately 52% of TTWO’s revenues.

From FY25 to FY27, 22 immersive core titles are expected to be released, including major names like Borderlands 4 and Mafia: The Old Country, which, in my view, will support the firm’s top line in the coming years.

Recurring Revenue as a Core Strategy

More and more video game companies have tried to implement game pass models and in-game purchases to provide a certain stability and predictability to revenues. The initial issue with AAA video games is that they take a long time to develop. Once a game is developed, it may generate a lot of revenue, with a spike at its release followed by a progressive decline over time. Then, new games must be developed, which consumes cash and can endanger the company if no other revenues are generated in the meantime.

In this context, the integration of Zynga makes a lot of sense, as it helps the company diversify its game portfolio and reach a global hyper-casual player base. This large potential customer pool is initially attracted to free-to-play games, generating engagement, potential retention, and then monetization. When the Zynga acquisition was announced in 2022, I found it interesting as a way to provide consistent cash flow to finance other initiatives.

Thus, it is worth noting that for FY 2024, 78% of TTWO’s revenues were related to recurring consumer spending, compared to 62% before the Zynga acquisition.

Valuation

I believe that TTWO is about to experience a major growth cycle driven by GTA VI and other titles set to launch in FY 2026. In this sense, I expect this release to boost TTWO’s top line by 40% in its release year, which is consistent with the past GTA V release. Aside from record sales, I expect digital purchases and GTA Online monetization to play a key role in the firm's growth. That’s why I expect FY 2027 to grow by 25%, supported by DLCs and a potential next-gen adoption. I also anticipate sustained growth for FY 2028, with a +10% top-line increase, and +7% top-line growth for FY 2029.

I expect gross margins to progressively return to 50% by FY 2029, with net income margins expanding toward 18-20%, driven mainly by higher digital revenue, direct-to-consumer sales, and new monetization strategies. In this scenario, I anticipate the console, PC & other segment to represent close to 70% of the firm's revenues, while the mobile segment will account for a smaller share than it does today. I expect this segment to grow at a mid-single-digit rate over the next five years.

All these assumptions lead me to expect an average 23% FCF growth rate over the next five years, which is consistent with TTWO’s FCF growth rate post-GTA V release, which was nearly 25%. Using a 2% perpetual growth rate and discounting with a 9.5% WACC, I reach a $263 target price, representing a 25% upside potential.

Risks

I acknowledge that TTWO represents a risky investment opportunity. All eyes are focused on GTA VI, which is understandable given the huge amount of money invested in this project. In this sense, I believe the main risk to consider when investing in TTWO is the potential for disappointment with the game's quality, similar to what we saw with the release of Cyberpunk 2077. In my opinion, given the time developers have had and Rockstar Games' track record, this is unlikely.

Another risk to consider is the potential decline in interest toward the GTA franchise. Even if the game is good, there is competition in the video game industry, and it is possible that gamers may choose to allocate their time to other games when GTA VI and other TTWO titles are released.

Finally, my scenario is based on a PC release occurring relatively early, one year after the console release, and a new console cycle starting in 2028, which would boost sales. Any factor contradicting these assumptions could potentially pose a risk to TTWO's growth and thus to my thesis.

Conclusion

Within the video game industry, which I have been following closely for many years, I consider TTWO one of the most promising opportunities given the quality of its franchises and studios. As shown, TTWO is now a diversified company with recurring revenues and a strong pipeline to support its growth.

Aside from that, I believe TTWO has the right culture. Video games are all about respecting players, bringing fun to them through great experiences. Many studios have forgotten this, but not TTWO, which is why the hype is so huge. Significant hype implies high potential, but it is also a double-edged sword in case of failure. I expect TTWO to deliver more than ever, and for GTA VI to represent a milestone in the history of video games.

I maintain my STRONG BUY rating on TTWO and will keep you updated as we get closer to autumn 2025. Fasten your seatbelts, we're heading to Vice City!

data.title: Why I'm Cautious On Rocket Lab Despite Its Strong Growth Story
data.description:
Executive Summary

After I spent some time looking at Rocket Lab's (NASDAQ:RKLB) latest finances and position in the market dynamics, I believe that, despite the tremendous execution of the company, the current valuation of $28.30 appears unsustainable for now. While doing my research, I found losses mounting and a staggering 38x P/S Ratio creating sufficient cessation in my mind to doubt the stock's near-term prospects. My detailed DCF analysis estimates a base case valuation of $16.20 per share, with a range of $12.40 to $33.00, accounting for various growth and execution scenarios. Sticking with optimistic assumptions about market growth and margin expansion, doing the math to justify a trading price current nearly at the bull case valuation range proved to be a challenge. I, too, respect the strength of the revenue growth and the strategic positioning of the company. The potent combination of expensive valuation multiples and significant cash burn leads toward the conclusion to trim exposure at these levels.

My Take on the Business Model

Rocket Lab is intrigued by the best approach to revenue on the basis of two distinct aspects. In the launch service division, I have gathered that Electron rocket programs between $7 million and $10 million per standard mission, and premium prices are offered for special launches. From tracked launches, I have also noted a steady increase in launch frequency to support their growth narrative.

What stands out for me quite convincingly is the Space Systems segment, as I consider it to be the most strategically critical unit of the firm. In my opinion, the division can gain a unique competitive advantage through the manufacturing of satellite components and spacecraft platforms. I've noticed that when customers turn to Rocket Lab for a launch, that usually ends with them wanting to buy additional components and services, forming what I see as a very powerful revenue flywheel.

Growth and Market Position

From what I have learned regarding their Q3 2024 reports, I was astounded by a 55% growth in revenue on a year-over-year basis to a stupendous 104.8 million! They also reported nine-month revenue of 303.8 million, an increase of 65% over 184.6 million in 2023. My market analysis led me to believe that these respective leaps were courtesy of broadening market opportunities and sustained execution of their vertical integration strategy.

What's interesting is the improving gross margins, which expanded to 26.7% in Q3 2024 from 22.1% in Q3 2023. I'm a bit concerned about operating cost explosion. During my analysis, I learned that R&D increased by 79%, totaling $47.7 million, while SG&A grew by 18%, reaching $32.2 million. Against the background of ongoing operational loss of $51.9 million, I think this growing trend in costs will become increasingly troublesome for the company.

Valuation Analysis and Assumptions

To build a valuation framework of Rocket Lab, I adopted multiple methodologies to triangulate a fair value range. My analysis began with the comparable company multiples, where I considered a number of space and defense companies including Northrop Grumman, L3Harris, and Lockheed Martin. These peers have an enterprise value to revenue multiples between 1.8x and 2.3x, with an average multiple of 2.1x. However, because Rocket Lab's growth rate of 55% against the peer mean of 8%, I think a growth premium deserves to be assigned. The scale width corresponding to this growth differential, I work back on my multiple of 2.8x to 3.0x, which suggests a value range of $16.20 to $17.40 a share when applied to Rocket Lab's trailing twelve months revenues of $363.8 million.

I also employed the SOTP valuation approach to anchor value across Rocket Lab's several business segments. The Launch Services, which makes approximately $200 million, should be assigned a 2.5x multiple because of open-market comparisons with pure play launch firms generating a value of $500 million. Meanwhile, the Space Systems, which has generated $163.8 million in revenues and a higher growth outlook, warrants a 3.0x multiple, corresponding to a value of $491.4 million. The total value works out to $991.4 million, or $16.80 per share.

While the DCF model suggests an equity value below zero due to short-term costs incurred as losses, the discounting cash flows model brings forth major discussions on Rocket Lab's path towards profitability and capital requirements. It estimates revenue growth from $363.8 million to a staggering $4.1 billion by 2034, that gross margins will widen to 40%, and that there will be tremendous improvements in operating leverage that will lead to overall free cash flow positivity down the road. Whereas a DCF works out to be a number, and while it is very difficult to arrive at a declining values number continuing in decreasing periods due to the infancy of the business, it concurs with the upper and lower sides of a valuation band that was supported by the other methodologies.

The convergence of the various approaches makes me confident in my fair value range of between $16.20 and $18.80 per share. Rocket Lab now trades at a whopping 62% premium to my midpoint value of $28.30 and a 51% premium to my high-end estimate, which represents nearly 3.2x 2024 estimated revenues. While acknowledging the company's great execution and market position, this analysis provides that the stock is overvalued at the current level.

Sensitivity Analysis Explained

My findings of the wide-ranging results ($12.40-$33.00 per share) in my sensitivity analysis indicate a high level of uncertainty, which entails forecasting cash flows by a company in a fast-evolving industry.

The bull case of $33.00 holds up under the following assumptions:

  • Faster market adoption of small satellite services.
  • The successful scaling of launching frequency over time.
  • A margin improvement that exceeds expectations.
  • Robust Growth in the Space Systems segment.

The bear case of $12.40 considers:

  • Increased competition, pressuring margins
  • Slower market growth
  • Higher capital requirement
  • Settlement of the technical challenges around scaling out operations

At the current price of $28.30, the market is pricing in bullish assumptions, requiring sustained high revenue growth, margin expansion, and strong execution.

Risks to Bearish Thesis

While I take things cautiously, I recognize that there could be some biases in my analysis. One of these biases is rapid development of the space market as one of the potential promoters I have recognized in my research. The number of satellite constellations and the ever-increasing space budgets will drive demand beyond current estimates.

I am also watching the operational efficiency of the company very closely. If management is able to execute better than I expect, and uses the opportunity for a margin-expansion as an edge with an eye toward vertical integration, we could see profitability accelerate faster than I have modeled. In my analysis of the industry, I have also seen Rocket Lab's strategic value could see them come under acquisition from larger aerospace companies or provide for significant transformative government contracts, which could really alter the tack of their growth path.

Looking Ahead

I will be focusing on several key metrics as their next earnings report approaches on February 27th. Based on my analysis, one of the most important indicators of the company moving towards profitability will be the operational losses in combination with the cash burn rate. Another area of interest will be their development through Neutron, which, I believe, could create additional market opportunities, but it seems the market is pricing in very bullish expectations with Neutron beforehand.

Conclusion

While I do appreciate Rocket Lab's expertise in building vertically integrated space companies, I find a valuation of $28.30 hard to justify for now. My valuation evaluation calls for this price to be applicable with almost flawless execution in an industry that is notoriously difficult. While I'm very positive in my long-term assessment of the company's prospects, I suggest reducing exposure at these levels. Investing in either meaningful operational improvements or a more attractive point of entry would be better for investors.

Recommendation: Trim Exposure

I believe that investors should await either significant operational improvement or a more attractive entry point before establishing or adding to positions in RKLB.

Note: All financial data sourced from company's Q3 2024 financial statements. Analysis reflects market conditions as of February 12, 2025.

data.title: Fear Fades, Fortunes Rise: British American's 10% Earnings Plunge Is A Buying Beacon
data.description:
Earnings season is famous for considerable volatility, and for British American Tobacco (NYSE:BTI) investors, this quarter is one of those.

Even famous dividend aristocrats can fall as much as 20% in a single day, and usually, it's an excellent buying opportunity.

While the sample size isn't huge, the results are pretty impressive.

  • Average 12-Month Forward Return: 34.74%
  • Median 12-Month Forward Return: 33.4%

In other words, one day, dividend aristocrat crashes can be a great way to earn Buffett-like returns on blue-chip bargains hidden in plain sight.

On the other hand, sometimes big crashes are a sign of the wheels falling off the bus. Let's not forget that V.F Corp (VFC), 3M (MMM), AT&T (T), and Walgreens (WBA) were all once aristocrats that crashed big but then kept right on crashing (WBA even suspended its dividend entirely).

And since 70%-plus of companies that crash never recover, it's essential to distinguish between a value trap and a Buffett-like "Fat pitch" opportunity.

Wait for a fat pitch and then swing for the fences." - Warren Buffett

At one point on Thursday, BTI was down almost 11%, the kind of day that many investors fear but others love.

Is this a chance for a 33% one-year profit? Or is this the beginning of the end for this venerable dividend aristocrat?

Bottom Line Up Front: I Just Bought 525 Shares of BTI (And Counting)

I update my yield-based limit orders for my core high-yield stocks whenever they raise their dividends (BTI just hiked 2%).

In addition to the -5% and -10% crash rules (for any core stocks I own), two of the limit orders were also filled, bringing my total number of BTI shares to almost 12,000.

BTI remains one of my most conventional ideas, and I'm thrilled to have bought so many shares at highly attractive valuations.

Fact One: Why Wall Street Punished British Americans so Brutally

Dividend Yield ~7.7% +2% (dividend)
Net Debt/EBITDA 2.44x 2.50X

(Source: BTI press release)

At first glance, negative sales growth and a decline in free cash flow are potentially alarming.

After all, in 2024, sales also declined (by 5%).

However, most of that is due to currency effects and one-time events, such as BTI exiting Russia and Belarus.

  • Currency-adjusted revenue was +1.3% in 2024.

While growth was below historical norms (more on that in a moment), it was despite numerous headwinds, including a $7 billion provision for a legal settlement in Canada and some major setbacks in Bangladesh and Australia.

Let me tell you about Bangladesh. Normally, you see an excise policy every mid year, June. So what happened is that the government, in the pressure to raise tax collection, they decided to do an ad hoc increase, not just in cigarettes, but they increased VAT and duties over 100 products, including cigarettes.

And then the next specific exercise increasing all tier points, including the above inflation, the floor price. So it's a question of really affordability because consumers were already struggling as many other consumers in the world with cost of living, food price increase, inflation of food price, and then on top of that had to face 100 products (that saw an) increase in price. And we had to increase across the board the price of cigarettes to cope with those excise increase. Now this is most of the 1% impact. There is also the Australia case that we highlighted.

But what we saw, and the Pakistan is a good example, when geographies go too far in terms of excise shocks, they will reflect that this is counterintuitive in terms of their desire to increase tax collection because what happened is exactly the opposite in the second year. After the establishment of a legal market, government tried to mitigate and take some actions to avoid this to happen. And this is our work assumption for 2026 that will be lapping in 'twenty five and not getting even further the impact in 'twenty six. Related to that, of course, the government in Bangladesh, 10% of overall tax collection is coming from tobacco, which means that they are very reliant on that. They cannot afford to see the spread of illegal markets across different years. And that's where we talk about the lapping in 2025." BTI CEO, Conference Call

Bangladesh raised its sales taxes (value-added tax) on many products to raise revenue, which caused an approximate 1% sales tailwind for BTI company wide.

Meanwhile, in April, Australia will implement stricter packaging laws, advertising restrictions, and higher excise taxes. These changes are part of broader global trends in which governments impose tighter controls on tobacco products to promote public health.

So, to summarize:

  • Negative headline growth (slow positive adjusted growth).
  • Scary high legal costs.
  • Regulatory headwinds in key markets.

Fact Two: Why Management Is Confident That The Long-Term Plan Remains On Track

As part of its credit rating system, S&P considers every kind of risk that might affect a company's ability to repay its debt in the future.

It rates more than 13,000 companies globally, and British American isn't just good at long-term risk management. It's in the top 130 companies on earth.
